data.title: JFrog Appoints Sigal Zarmi to Board of Directors
data.description:
The latest announcement is out from JFrog ( (FROG) ).

On September 19, 2025, JFrog Ltd. announced the appointment of Sigal Zarmi to its Board of Directors, effective November 1, 2025. Zarmi, a seasoned CIO and digital transformation executive with experience at companies like Morgan Stanley and PwC, is expected to drive strategic enterprise growth in the areas of DevOps, DevSecOps, and MLOps. Her appointment comes amid significant shifts in the enterprise software market, and her expertise is anticipated to bolster JFrog’s position as a leader in the software and AI-driven world.

The most recent analyst rating on (FROG) stock is a Buy with a $55.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on JFrog stock, see the FROG Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on FROG Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FROG is a Outperform.

However, profitability challenges and valuation concerns due to negative earnings weigh on the score. The company’s strategic partnerships and leadership confidence provide additional support, but macroeconomic uncertainties and cloud adoption challenges remain risks.

To see Spark’s full report on FROG stock, click here.

More about JFrog

JFrog Ltd. (Nasdaq: FROG) is a company specializing in the development of a unified platform for DevOps, DevSecOps, and MLOps. Their mission is to enable seamless software delivery from development to production through their ‘Liquid Software’ vision. The JFrog Software Supply Chain Platform offers a secure, traceable, and tamper-proof system for managing and distributing software. With a hybrid, universal, multi-cloud platform, JFrog serves millions of users and over 7,000 customers worldwide, including many Fortune 100 companies.

data.title: Newbury Street Extends Business Combination Deadline
data.description:
The latest announcement is out from Newbury Street ( (NBST) ).

On September 24, 2025, Newbury Street Acquisition Corporation held a special meeting where stockholders approved two key proposals: the Extension Proposal and the Adjournment Proposal. The Extension Proposal allows the company to extend the deadline for completing a business combination from September 25, 2025, to March 25, 2026, while the Adjournment Proposal permits the meeting to be adjourned if necessary. Following the approval, the company amended its Certificate of Incorporation and processed redemptions for 5,590 shares, resulting in a payout of approximately $64,503.91.

More about Newbury Street

data.title: Intellia Therapeutics Announces Positive Phase 1 Trial Results
data.description:
The latest update is out from Intellia Therapeutics ( (NTLA) ).

On September 25, 2025, Intellia Therapeutics announced positive longer-term data from its ongoing Phase 1 trial of nexiguran ziclumeran (nex-z) for hereditary transthyretin amyloidosis with polyneuropathy. The trial showed deep, durable TTR reductions and favorable safety data, with most patients experiencing stability or improvement in clinical measures. The company is advancing its Phase 3 MAGNITUDE-2 trial, with completion of enrollment expected in the first half of 2026, and plans to submit a biologics license application by 2028.

The most recent analyst rating on (NTLA) stock is a Buy with a $25.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Intellia Therapeutics stock, see the NTLA Stock Forecast page.

However, the company’s financial performance is challenged by high R&D expenses and negative cash flows, typical for a biotech firm in development stages. The valuation reflects current unprofitability, and while the earnings call was positive, it highlighted some operational risks.

To see Spark’s full report on NTLA stock, click here.

More about Intellia Therapeutics

Intellia Therapeutics, Inc. is a leading clinical-stage gene editing company focused on developing CRISPR-based therapies to address unmet medical needs. The company aims to revolutionize medicine by leveraging gene editing technology to create novel, first-in-class medicines, particularly in the field of transthyretin amyloidosis.

data.title: Applied Energetics Appoints Retired General to Advisory Board
data.description:
Applied Energetics ( (AERG) ) has provided an announcement.

On September 25, 2025, Applied Energetics, Inc. announced the appointment of Brigadier General (Retired) Samuel “Luke” Peterson to its Advisory Board. With nearly 35 years of military service and extensive experience in defense acquisition, Peterson’s role is expected to bolster the company’s mission to advance ultrashort pulse laser and directed energy technologies for the Department of Defense. His past leadership in critical modernization initiatives and strategic planning for high-energy laser and hypersonic weapon systems aligns with Applied Energetics’ focus on delivering transformational capabilities. This appointment is anticipated to enhance the company’s industry positioning and accelerate the deployment of innovative technologies to safeguard national security.

More about Applied Energetics

Applied Energetics, Inc. specializes in advanced laser and photonics systems, particularly fiber-based ultrashort pulse (USP) laser technologies. The company holds 27 patents and 5 patents pending, focusing on delivering high intensity, ultrashort pulses of light for defense and commercial applications. Their technologies are designed to enhance national security by providing flexible and powerful solutions for complex missions.

data.description:
The latest update is out from Klotho Neurosciences ( (KLTO) ).

On September 19, 2025, Klotho Neurosciences, Inc. received a delinquency notification from Nasdaq due to its common stock failing to maintain a minimum bid price of $1 per share for 30 consecutive business days.
